commands.review_summary:
This PR updates the ClawHub CLI error formatter and top-level fallback to classify fetch/curl/DNS/TLS/proxy/timeout failures with redacted guidance, plus formatter regression tests.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible, not live-reproduced in this read-only review. Current main's formatter returns raw `error.message`, and the linked issue plus PR proof show the reported `clawhub search` path surfacing raw `fetch failed`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is one narrow, mechanical redaction repair that automation can attempt on the PR branch; maintainer review is still needed before final merge because the surface is security-sensitive.

target.title: fix(clawhub): explain CLI transport failures
target.body:
Closes #651.

Adds shared CLI transport diagnostics so fetch/curl/DNS/TLS/proxy/timeout failures produce actionable, redacted guidance instead of raw `fetch failed` output. Keeps ordinary HTTP/status errors concise and unchanged.

Hardens the diagnostic path so structured object causes and top-level CLI fallbacks cannot serialize auth headers, cookies, token values, or credential URLs.

Proof:
- Built the CLI and ran a real `clawhub search` against `https://clawhub-proof.invalid`; terminal output reports `Network request failed: DNS lookup failed.` plus registry/proxy/TLS guidance, with no auth/token/cookie/credential URL values.
target.author: turbotheturtle
